The 60-day deadline for seeking authorization is approaching since the first strikes on February 28, with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth suggesting a pause during ceasefires.
This clarification could impact U.S. military strategy in Iran and the administration's accountability to Congress.

stories.title: One NZ issue causing widespread internet outage across entire South, parts of North Island
bullets: OneNZ and 2degrees customers reported widespread internet outages across the South Island and parts of the North Island on May 1, 2026.
The outages affected both internet services and mobile calls, disrupting communication for thousands of users.
The incident highlights vulnerabilities in telecommunications infrastructure and the need for improved service reliability.

stories.title: Rotorua fatal bus crash: Woman charged over alleged nang cover-up
bullets: A woman has been charged in connection with a fatal bus crash in Rotorua, as of May 1, 2026.
Police allege she concealed a nitrous oxide canister from the crashed vehicle, raising concerns about substance use.
This case underscores ongoing issues related to road safety and the dangers of impaired driving.

stories.title: Samsung Chip Profits Soar Amid the Tech World's RAM Shortages
bullets: Samsung's chip profits are surging due to ongoing RAM shortages in the tech industry, driven by AI compute demand.
The company reported a 30% increase in profits for Q1 2026, capitalizing on the supply constraints affecting competitors.
As AI technology continues to grow, Samsung's position may strengthen, impacting market dynamics and pricing strategies.

stories.title: How Shivon Zilis Operated as Elon Musk’s OpenAI Insider
bullets: Shivon Zilis, Elon Musk's partner and mother of four of his children, acted as a liaison with OpenAI, revealed during a recent trial.
Messages disclosed show Zilis facilitated communications between Musk and OpenAI, raising questions about conflicts of interest and corporate governance.
This revelation could impact Musk's reputation and OpenAI's operational transparency, influencing future partnerships and public trust.

stories.title: Apple Expects 'Significantly Higher Memory Costs' to Impact iPhone, MacBook Neo
bullets: Apple anticipates 'significantly higher memory costs' affecting the pricing of the iPhone 17 and MacBook Neo due to RAM shortages.
The company expects a 15% increase in production costs, which may lead to higher retail prices for consumers.
This situation could impact sales and customer demand, as consumers weigh the cost against the benefits of new devices.

stories.title: Sony Future Filmmaker Awards Announce 2026 Shortlisted Finalists
bullets: The Sony Future Filmmaker Awards announced its 2026 shortlist, featuring finalists across five categories, including fiction and animation.
Selected filmmakers will participate in exclusive sessions at Sony Pictures Studios from June 8-11, culminating in a gala ceremony.
This initiative aims to promote emerging talent in filmmaking, enhancing Sony's commitment to the arts and entertainment industry.

stories.title: Watch The Magnetic Fields and Fleet Foxes Perform “Book of Love” Together
bullets: Stephen Merritt and Robin Pecknold performed 'Book of Love' live on East Village Radio's 'Elia Vs' show on May 1, 2026.
The collaboration highlighted the enduring popularity of the 1999 track, drawing significant listener engagement during the broadcast.
This performance may lead to increased interest in both artists' upcoming projects and collaborations in the indie music scene.

stories.title: Transgender Idahoans sue over bathroom law
bullets: Six transgender residents of Idaho filed a lawsuit against the state on April 30, 2026, challenging a new bathroom law.
The law prohibits transgender individuals from using sex-designated restrooms, prompting the ACLU and Lambda Legal to represent the plaintiffs.
The outcome of this lawsuit could set a precedent for transgender rights and bathroom access laws across the United States.

stories.title: U.S. indictment of sitting Mexican governor puts Sheinbaum in a bind
bullets: Mexican Governor Rubén Rocha Moya of Sinaloa faces U.S. indictment for alleged ties to a drug cartel, announced on May 1, 2026.
The indictment raises serious concerns about corruption and governance in Mexico, with Rocha Moya denying all allegations.
This situation complicates political dynamics in Mexico, potentially affecting upcoming elections and international relations.

stories.title: From smoking to stigma: How screen stories influence health
bullets: Three individuals were rescued after their boat sank near Waitangi in the Bay of Islands on May 1, 2026.
Emergency services responded promptly, with all three victims reported safe and unharmed following the incident.
This event underscores the importance of maritime safety and preparedness in New Zealand's coastal regions.

stories.title: Three people rescued after boat sinks in Bay of Islands
bullets: Screen portrayals of smoking have historically influenced public perception, as seen with icons like James Dean in the 1950s.
Research indicates that such depictions can significantly affect smoking rates among youth, linking media representation to health behaviors.
Understanding this impact is crucial for public health campaigns aimed at reducing smoking prevalence and changing societal norms.

stories.title: ‘That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea’ Review: A Franchise Bonus Primarily for Fans
bullets: The anime film 'That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime: Tears of the Azure Sea' received mixed reviews, primarily appealing to franchise fans.
Critics noted the absence of expected elements from the series, which may disappoint newcomers unfamiliar with the storyline.
This film's reception could influence future adaptations and the direction of the franchise in the competitive anime market.

stories.title: Tim Cook reveals the advice he gave Apple’s next CEO: The most important decision he’ll make is ‘where he spends his time’
bullets: Tim Cook introduced incoming CEO John Ternus during Apple's quarterly earnings call, emphasizing strategic time management as crucial for leadership.
The company forecasts strong results for Q3, reflecting confidence in Ternus's ability to lead Apple forward.
This transition marks a significant moment for Apple, as Ternus will shape the company's future direction and innovation.

stories.title: Diablo 4 interactive map
bullets: An interactive map for Diablo 4 was released on May 1, 2026, detailing waypoints and Altars of Lilith for players.
The map enhances gameplay by providing strategic locations, improving player navigation and exploration experiences.
This tool is expected to boost player engagement and retention as they explore the game's vast world.

stories.title: My dad needed a new desktop PC - here's why this Dell was the smartest choice
bullets: A new 24-inch Dell AiO desktop was recommended for its compact design and reliable performance on May 1, 2026.
The desktop features high-quality speakers but has some limitations, including upgradeability and gaming performance.
This choice reflects a growing trend towards compact, efficient computing solutions for everyday users.

stories.title: The memory trade is faltering as Western Digital’s stock slides in the face of earnings beat
bullets: Western Digital's stock is declining despite beating earnings expectations, reflecting investor skepticism after a year of stock rallies.
The company's recent performance did not satisfy market expectations, leading to a sell-off in shares.
This trend may indicate a broader concern about the sustainability of growth in the memory market.

stories.title: All medallions in Far Far West Canyon and how to find them
bullets: A guide was published detailing the locations of four medallions in the game Far Far West Canyon, crucial for a secret.
Finding all medallions unlocks a secret door, rewarding players with Gold and an exclusive Joker card.
This discovery enhances gameplay experience, encouraging players to explore and complete challenges for rewards.

stories.title: A hobbyist mounted a cesium atomic clock on his Raspberry Pi
bullets: A hobbyist known as Chip Overclock mounted a cesium atomic clock on a Raspberry Pi, enhancing timekeeping accuracy.
This setup allows the clock to maintain precision within microseconds of UTC, even without GPS signal, addressing previous limitations.
The innovation showcases the potential for DIY technology projects to achieve professional-grade accuracy in timekeeping.

stories.title: In today’s Washington, even state dinners are parties for one party
bullets: At a recent state dinner for Britain's royals, only Democrats were invited, highlighting partisan divides in Washington.
The dinner, attended by President Biden and key Democratic figures, excluded prominent Republicans, raising eyebrows about political inclusivity.
This event underscores the growing polarization in U.S. politics, affecting even traditional diplomatic gatherings.

stories.title: How to get a discounted TSA PreCheck membership, starting May 1
bullets: Starting May 1, 2026, the TSA is offering discounted PreCheck memberships for qualifying customers.
The reduced price aims to encourage more travelers to enroll, enhancing airport security efficiency.
This initiative could lead to shorter security lines and improved travel experiences for frequent flyers.

stories.title: Best Outdoor Gear 2026: Top-Rated Running Shoes, Hiking Boots, and Layers Reviewed
bullets: The Scotsman reviewed top-rated outdoor gear for 2026, including running shoes and hiking boots, on May 1, 2026.
The review highlights products that cater to spring activities, reflecting seasonal consumer trends.
This guide aims to assist outdoor enthusiasts in making informed purchasing decisions for the upcoming season.

stories.title: The Best Horror Movie From Every Year in the 2000s
bullets: Summary unavailable

stories.title: Amal Salad sentenced for causing fatal crash that killed promising young jockey, Ngakau Hailey
bullets: Amal Salad was sentenced for causing a crash that killed jockey Ngakau Hailey in New Zealand on May 1, 2026.
Hailey was performing a wheelie on his dirt bike when Salad failed to yield, resulting in a fatal collision.
This case highlights the dangers of reckless driving and may prompt stricter traffic regulations in New Zealand.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US ports siege ‘intolerable’; Trump mulls action
bullets: Tehran condemned the US ports siege as 'intolerable' amid escalating tensions following Israeli attacks on Lebanon.
Since March 2, Israeli strikes have killed at least 2,586 people and injured over 8,000, according to local media.
The situation raises concerns about potential military responses from the US and further destabilization in the region.

stories.title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
bullets: Tim Cook announced record sales for Apple as he prepares to step down, with revenues hitting $394 billion.
However, he warned of supply-chain challenges from a RAM shortage, potentially impacting future product availability.
This transition period could affect investor confidence and Apple's market strategy as it navigates upcoming challenges.

stories.title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
bullets: Skio, a subscription billing fintech, was sold to competitor Recharge for $105 million cash after raising only $8 million.
The founder described the sale as a healthy exit, indicating strong market interest in subscription-based financial services.
This acquisition may lead to increased competition in the fintech sector, particularly in subscription billing solutions.

stories.title: Jihadists urge united front against Mali junta as Bamako blockade begins
bullets: Jihadist fighters in Mali called for a united uprising against the military junta as a blockade on Bamako begins.
This appeal follows coordinated attacks on junta positions and raises fears of further instability in the conflict-ridden nation.
The situation could escalate into broader violence, complicating international efforts to stabilize Mali.

stories.title: Moscow’s ambitions face grim prospects in Mali after military defeat
bullets: Russian troops withdrew from several cities in Mali after facing significant attacks from Islamic insurgents on April 30, 2026.
The insurgents targeted military bases, resulting in heavy casualties and forcing a strategic retreat by Russian forces.
This defeat complicates Russia's ambitions in Africa, potentially impacting its influence and military presence in the region.
